title: Interesting Review of Evangelion You Are Not Alone at ANN
display_title: Interesting Review of Evangelion You Are Not Alone at ANN
wikitext:
[]: Interesting Review of Evangelion: You Are (Not) Alone at ANN             Quote Post by The Goose » Thu Jul 10, 2008 4:06 pm http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/review/evangelion-1.0-you-are-not-alone My favorite part of it was: Newcomers to the franchise (provided they actually exist) will have a good time but would probably be better served by just watching the original TV series and then waiting along with the rest of us for Rebuild of Evangelion 4.0: Now We're Finally At The Good Part.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Thu Jul 10, 2008 4:32 pm review wrote: who now has a cool new abdomen made up of hundreds of pairs of human legs

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Jul 10, 2008 5:29 pm I actually thought Rebuild 1.0 was a really nice re-animation of a lot of stuff, a good update of episodes 1 to 6, and a warning that "next movie we're going to start changing things drastically" But like, stuff that wasn't animated well in the original episodes 1 to 6 finally got a good treatment. And YES, the new younger generation of Naruto addicts often haven't actually seen the entire series.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 10, 2008 6:36 pm I posted a response: DrizzlingEnthalpy wrote: The review was wrong about several things. If you look at the character designs side-by-side, they have indeed been changed a bit. The best comparison you can see is Rei's smiling scene compared to that of the original series and Death . The backgrounds and music are all redone as well. The reviewer said they were the same as before, but the backgrounds are much more detailed and in many places quite different. The music, while usually remixing older scores, is played by a better orchestra. The only song that was really pretty much the same as its decade-old counterpart was Decisive Battle , which did also have a little more percussion. Furthermore, it's not necessarily that they're being a lot more upfront this time, it's that the mysteries aren't necessarily the same. For example, did you notice that all the parts about Second Impact's coverup were removed, as well as the scenes that seem to imply whose soul is in Unit 01?

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Thu Jul 10, 2008 8:28 pm I kinda figured that response was from someone on this forum.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Jul 11, 2008 1:48 pm ANN... Maiking breakthrough, as usual.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jul 11, 2008 2:10 pm Zac is actually rather intelligent guy and his reviews (and taste ) tends to be good. His wrong on some things but to be honest I too for long time saw no difference in the soundtracks.

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Fri Jul 11, 2008 4:15 pm ANN... Maiking breakthrough, as usual.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 13, 2008 11:11 am Changed character designs? Depends on which scenes you're looking at... It's not as if the movie is totally consistent. Part of this comes from reusing NGE material, but they could have updated all of the art if they really wanted to.

[]:             Quote Post by Zerogoki » Sun Jul 13, 2008 12:48 pm for many Evangelion fans, the fact that the entire cast was slowly revealed to be a pack of selfish, emotionally mangled mental patients was half the fun.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Mon Jul 14, 2008 10:17 am Zerogoki wrote: for many Evangelion fans, the fact that the entire cast was slowly revealed to be a pack of selfish, emotionally mangled mental patients was half the fun.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Jul 14, 2008 11:48 am NGE is one of Zac's favourite animes. But he is casual fan, not hardcore I don't see what's bugging you guys so much about the review. It was best one I've so far seen about movie

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Jul 16, 2008 3:09 pm The Goose wrote: ANN... Maiking breakthrough, as usual.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Fri Jul 18, 2008 7:55 am as a new user I'd like to make a short comment about RoE first movie. :) I've seen the original series a hundred times and EoE really countless times, therefore my perception of the new movie can be biased. But, I think it's an almost perfect retouch and a great introduction to the next films. 1. The fun parts were cut to the minimum. Finally we have an apocaliptic world without to much jokes and typical for anime comedy cliches, like Misato's car being magically bounced from left to right on the road. A marvelous change. 2. Characters. I know, some think they are dull, some think they are way to emotional without any backing. I watched the movie few times and I can say that they are almost perfectly balanced. Shinji. We see from the beginning that he's a shy and calm guy AND, what wasn't clearly showed in the series, he hasn't got anything against being alone. He seems to be more emotionally stable than in the original episodes. The scene when he encounters his father and the Eva o1 gives a quick glance on his first dilemma. To be a pilot against his will or reject the job but disappoint lots of people. That is the main subject of this movie. Later on he discovers that there may be more options because the world's existance is at stake. Again a marvelous move, because noone goes sunbathing on Sahara without a good cream with high UV protection, if you know what I mean. It's a movie ALSO for newcomers, and don't forget the original series hasn't got much philospohical stuff in the first episodes either. So the cuts on Shinji were made just fine. What's more, the completly new scene in the baths with Misato after the battle with Shamshel, which was done somewhat in b&w style (great), shows that Shinji is more mature than we thought. Lots of people taunted Shinji for being a crybaby. I think in the movie his character was changed or rather showed differently. In the movie, he's a normal boy angry at his father and with a simple dillema. For now. Other characters were showed clear of problems. IMO it must have been like that, because the first movie is just a prelude and the main subject for this short flick was to intruduce us to the world of Evangelion. 3. HCP and the out of reality conversations were showed just few times and all of them were fixed in one place. Running train. It couldn't be better. If they keep it, than we'll have two plots going on and the philosphical part will be done as a classical tragedy with the three unities of action, place and time. That would be awesome. 4. The action itself was nice, and the last battle was just perfect. It's one of those motives I feel that I couldn't make any better. The music, old and new, absolutely outstanding and blended with the plot as good as possible. All in all, as a renovated introduction and prelude the RoE 1.0 gets almost a perfect 10/10. It must be marked that high because the original series is a solid 9/10. :) To make it more on-topic I'll say I don't agree with the article.

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Fri Jul 18, 2008 8:05 am Pierce that wall of text with your drill, Simon! Please to be giving a TL;DR version kthx.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Jul 18, 2008 8:07 am I thought pictures in sigs were banned under pain of Reichu-related death.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Fri Jul 18, 2008 12:40 pm The Bastard King wrote: Pierce that wall of text with your drill, Simon! Please to be giving a TL;DR version kthx.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 18, 2008 1:00 pm That wasn't long enough to qualify as a "Wall of Text". Take some Adderall, you Bastard. @AnimeNFO: "Neon Genisis", oh lawl.

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Sat Jul 19, 2008 9:29 pm I've been staying away from Webuild discussion for now, since I'm still waiting on an official R1 release that I can watch; I know it's a little more complicated than that, and I could be waiting a damn long while, but I've just not been keeping up with the stuff. ...from Zac's summary, it doesn't seem like 1.0 captures what I liked about the series. I like NGE because it was flawed and perhaps unappetizing to some. I'd certainly call the discovery of the cast"fun", by the way. :)

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Sat Jul 19, 2008 9:34 pm JimmyWolk wrote: review wrote: EVA-01 now has an awesome new roar
